rulings.Case Title: CENTURY SURETY COMPANY VS. SAN CARLOS ROOFING, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, DECEMBER 3, 2007, LINE 1. PLAINTIFF CENTURY SURETY COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Century's moving papers do not establish as an undisputed fact that the fire was caused by "any other heated substance." Two causes are undisputed - the misuse of [Radoycis'] torch and his failure to conduct a proper "fire watch". (Fact 10.) Although when modified bitumen is heated it becomes molten, Century has not posited as an undisputed fact that the heated bitumen caused the fire. To the extent Century seeks to have the Court determine that "the misuse of [Radoycis'] torch in performing 'torch down roofing' constitutes "any other heated substance", the Court finds that the policy language is ambiguous. Once that finding is made, defendant/cross-complainant has presented facts that Radoycis' reasonable expectation was that the "any other heated substance" exclusion did not exclude the conduct at issue. There also are triable issues of fact as to whether the application constitutes a misrepresentation fact based on the Declarations of Radoycis and Peterson. The Court grants Century's motion for summary adjudication of the bad faith cause of action. Century's interpretation of the policy as to these facts is not unreasonable as a matter of law. The Court will issue its ruling on the evidentiary objections at the hearing. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: UNION PAPER BOX & PRINTING PRESS LIMITED VS. ARTISTIC STUDIOS, LTD., LLC A COLORADO LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, DECEMBER 3, 2007, LINE 6. CROSS-DEFENDANTS UNION PAPER BOX & PRINTING PRESS, LIMITED, AND SEAWICK ENTERPRISES, LIMITED'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS- COMPLAINT: Demurrer sustained in part and overruled in part: 1st and 2nd causes of action, contract claims, overruled; 3rd and 4th causes of action, breach of warranty, sustained, no opposition filed and the claims as stated are barred by the statute of limitations; 5th cause of action, negligent interference, sustained; plaintiff is given leave to amend to alleged facts giving rise to a duty independent of the performance of the terms of the contract; 6th cause, intentional interference, is overruled based on paragraph 56; 7th cause of action, unfair business practices, is sustained with leave to amend to clarify what is meant by "license design" to enable the court and the parties to determine if the copyright laws are applicable; 8th cause of action, alter ego, pleads conclusions, not facts to support such a claim, leave to amend is granted; and 9th cause of action, conversion, overruled.Ten days leave to amend is granted. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: JANET STANFORD VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SHERIFF'S DEPT. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn To Require Pl To Furnish Security Ccp 391.1 And To Enter Pre-Filing Order Pursuant To Ccp391.7, Request For Judicial Ntc In Support
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, DECEMBER 3, 2007, LINE 14. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND SHERIFF'S DEPARTMENT'S MOTION To Require PLAINTIFF To Furnish Security CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 391.1 And To Enter Pre-Filing Order Pursuant To CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 391.7 (Request For Judicial NOTICE) IS GRANTED. THE COURT DECLARES PLAINTIFF TO BE A VEXATIOUS LITIGANT UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 391(B)(1), (B)(2) AND (B)(3). THERE IS NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT PLAINTIFF WILL PREVAIL IN THIS LITIGATION. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO FURNISH SECURITY WITH THE COURT IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,000.00 ON OR BEFORE DECEMBER 15, 2007 OR THIS ACTION WILL BE DISMISSED WITH PREJUDICE. THE COURT ENTERS A PRE-FILING ORDER PROHIBITING PLAINTIFF FROM FILING ANY NEW LITIGATION IN PROPERIA PERSONA WITHOUT FIRST OBTAINING LEAVE OF THE PRESIDING JUDGE. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: OLIVER HILSENRATH et al VS. MIKE SHEPARD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, DECEMBER 3, 2007, LINE 18. DEFENDANTS MIKE SHEPARD AND HELLER EHRMAN, L.L.P.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PENDING ARBITRATION IS GRANTED. THE AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE IS VALID AND ENFORCEABLE. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT IT IS A CONTRACT OF ADHESION. IT APPEARS THAT MS. HILSENRATH DOES NOT HAVE STANDING TO ASSERT CLAIMS AGAINST DEFENDANT AND IF SHE DID, HER CLAIMS MAY BE BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. IT ALSO APPEARS THAT HER CLAIMS ARE DERIVATIVE OF OLIVER HILSENRATH'S CLAIMS. ACCORDINGLY, PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO STAY MS. HILSENRATH'S CLAIMS PENDING THE CONCLUSION OF THE ARBITRATION IS GRANTED. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration

ROYAL'S FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 3.1330 IS TROUBLING, PARTICULARLY IN LIGHT OF THE REQUIREMENT THAT THE TVIAKI ACTION REMAINS UNRESOLVED WITH A PENDING TRIAL DATE. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: JACQUELINE PIERI VS. CHRISTOPHER DOLAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, DECEMBER 3, 2007, LINE 22. PLAINTIFF JACQUELINE PIERI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION on the issue of duty is granted in part and denied in part. It is denied on the issue of a duty to vacate because there are triable issues of fact for plaintiff's motivation for invoking the Ellis Act and triable issues of fact as to certain of the affirmative defenses: 9th based on the uncertainty as to why Rock Ploichcik is performing certain services; 10th based on the same issue as the 9th; and the 11th based on plaintiff's motivation for invoking the Ellis Act. The motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the following defenses:1st, failure to comply based on the contention that Ms. Pieri is not Ms. Plichchcik is without merit; 2nd, improper service is without merit; 5th, rent increase not relevant to duty to vacate or damages; 6th, retaliatory rent increase not relevant to duty to vacate or damages; 7th and 13th, estoppel not relevant to the duty to vacate or damages; and 8th, waiver, not accepting rent is a condition of the Ellis Act and cannot constitute waiver. Plaintiff seeks hold over damages for failure to vacate, not rent. The following defenses present triable issues of fact on the issue of damages: 3rd, payment of relocation benefits and 4th, habitability. =(302/PJM/AY)
